task_completion.description: sumo-gui is open and active with the road network loaded
task_completion.visual_evidence: The sumo-gui application window is visible on the screen, displaying a road network (Bologna Acosta).

task_completion.description: The visualization/view settings dialog is opened
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'View Settings' or similar configuration dialog box is open over the sumo-gui main window, typically showing tabs for Vehicles, Streets, etc.

task_completion.description: Vehicle coloring is configured to 'by speed'
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the view settings dialog (likely under the Vehicles tab), the color option dropdown is set to 'by speed' or a similar speed-based coloring scheme.

task_completion.description: The simulation is running and vehicles are colored by speed
task_completion.visual_evidence: The simulation time step is greater than 0 (or the play button is active), and vehicles are visible on the road network rendered in different colors corresponding to their speeds.

task_completion.description: A screenshot of the visualization is saved to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal window shows a command like 'scrot /home/ga/SUMO_Output/speed_coloring.png' being executed, or a screenshot tool's save dialog shows the file being saved to exactly '/home/ga/SUMO_Output/speed_coloring.png'.

integrity.description: Agent used the sumo-gui to change visualization settings
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the sumo-gui 'View Settings' dialog rather than editing SUMO configuration XML files in a text editor.

integrity.description: The simulation is genuinely running in sumo-gui
integrity.visual_evidence: The sumo-gui interface should show standard simulation controls, a progressing time step, and rendered vehicles, rather than an image viewer displaying a static picture.

integrity.description: The screenshot was actively taken by the agent
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be evidence of the agent invoking a screenshot utility (like scrot in the terminal) with the exact required path, rather than just moving or renaming an existing file.
